data_modeling.description: Shape, constraints, and indexes of persistent data — how information is stored, retrieved, and evolves.

api_design.description: Public surface contracts — protocols, versioning, error shapes, and evolvability across clients.

auth_and_authz.description: Who the caller is (authentication) and what they may do (authorization); identity provisioning and session handling.

input_validation.description: Safety of data crossing trust boundaries — sanitization, schema enforcement, and defense against injection.

caching.description: Keeping frequently-used data close to the caller; invalidation, layering, and staleness trade-offs.

async_processing.description: Work that executes outside the request path — queues, streams, schedulers, and event-driven pipelines.

error_handling.description: Graceful response to failures — exceptions, fallbacks, degraded modes, and user-visible error semantics.

observability.description: Instrumentation that makes system behavior visible — logs, metrics, traces, and their correlation.

rate_limiting.description: Protecting systems and callers from overload — throttling, quotas, backpressure, and fairness.

data_consistency.description: Correctness across concurrent writes, replicas, and distributed transactions; isolation and conflict resolution.

service_integration.description: How components communicate across process/network boundaries — protocols, coupling, and contract boundaries.

deployment_strategy.description: Getting code to production safely — pipelines, rollouts, rollbacks, and environment parity.

schema_migration.description: Evolving persistent schemas and APIs without breaking in-flight clients or existing data.

scalability.description: Handling increasing load — horizontal/vertical growth, partitioning, load distribution, and capacity.

security_and_secrets.description: Cryptographic primitives, key/secret management, threat modeling, and defense-in-depth posture.

cost_optimization.description: Resource efficiency and tier selection — spending less to achieve the same functional and non-functional targets.

sli_slo_sla.description: Reliability targets and error budgets — what 'good enough' means quantitatively and how to spend unreliability.

testing_strategy.description: What to test and at what level — unit/integration/e2e balance, test doubles, and verification of behavior under stress.

idempotency_and_retry.description: Safe repetition of operations under failure — exactly-once-ish semantics, retry budgets, and idempotency keys.
canonical_sources: AWS Builders' Library 'Challenges with distributed systems'

orphan_l1s.arrays: Implementation-level data structure; serves as L2 parent in Phase 2/3 (e.g., L2 'sparse_arrays', 'ring_buffer')
